verdict: PASS — all hard checks pass; one soft finding (undefined term 'pharmacopeia' at line 75); does not reach escalation threshold.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is '# The Judgment Framework — Q1: What is the cost of this being wrong?' — correct H1, no leading frontmatter. All 6 required sections present in the required order.

length.notes: 1347 words at 200 wpm = 6.7 min (rounded to 7 in sidecar). Within standard profile target of 1200–1600 words and 6–8 minutes.

citations.notes: [1] aicompetence.org (primary) cited multiple times including Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways. [2] arXiv 2508.03714 cited throughout. [3] corporatefinanceinstitute.com cited in In Practice and Key Takeaways. Bibliography matches all inline markers.

diagram_embedded.notes: Inline image referencing diagram.png appears in Key Concepts section at line 13 with descriptive alt text.

deferred_concepts_check.Q2_treatment: bare name + topic pointer only — 'Q2 and Q3 are introduced in topics 9.8 and 9.9' (line 9) and 'Q2 (topic 9.8) covers exactly how to verify' (line 75). Not taught or defined.
deferred_concepts_check.Q3_treatment: bare name + topic pointer only. Not taught or defined.
deferred_concepts_check.notes: Anchoring bias (topic 9.4) referenced in In Practice section as a previously-taught concept — legitimate back-reference, not a forward reference or out-of-scope teaching.

scope.notes: Topic boundary is Q1 only. Q2 and Q3 are named with topic pointers but not taught. No deferred concept is bolded, defined, or receives an appositive. No scope_creep found.

findings.detail: 'pharmacopeia' is used without definition in Worked Example Step 5: 'verify the dosage against the pharmacopeia and the prescribing physician's notes'. This is a non-everyday specialist term that a beginner (zero background) would not know. It should be replaced with plain language (e.g., 'the official drug reference guide') or defined inline.

define_before_use.notes: All key terms (Judgment Framework, Severity, Probability, Detectability) are defined with bold-term / plain-meaning shape on first use. 'System 2 thinking' is a prior-topic concept (9.2) and may be used without re-defining. Only 'pharmacopeia' (line 75) is unexplained — count 1, threshold is >2 to fail.

jargon_dump.notes: Three dimensions (Severity, Probability, Detectability) are each introduced in separate sub-sections, never stacked in a single paragraph. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously.

concrete_first.notes: Overview leads with two concrete scenarios (email sentence, nurse drug dosage) before stating the abstract framework principle. Each dimension sub-section leads with a bold definition then concrete examples or spectrum. Concrete-first pattern is consistently applied.

academic_register.notes: No instances of 'it is generally considered', 'many experts believe', 'various studies have shown', or equivalent hedging found. Register is plain, direct, and uses 'you' throughout.

wall_of_text.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences. Most paragraphs are 2–4 sentences. Bullet lists used throughout for the probability dimension, detectability bands, and the In Practice domain summaries.

unlisted_sequence.notes: Worked Example uses numbered steps (Step 1 through Step 6). Triage checklist in Key Concepts uses a numbered list. Do/Don't checklist in In Practice uses a table. No multi-step procedure rendered as run-on prose.

no_inline_examples.notes: Each dimension sub-section contains inline examples (e.g., severity spectrum with 'delete a wrong word', detectability with 'a number that is clearly impossible', 'a clause in a contract'). Inline examples appear throughout, not only in the Worked Example section.

definition_shape.notes: Severity, Probability, and Detectability all follow the bold-term / plain-meaning shape on first use (lines 18, 24, 39).

assessment_references.notes: No mention of A3, 'Maths and Cognition Lab Report', due weeks, grade weights, or any assessment ID found in the reading prose.

sampled_assertions.claim: Q1 asks you to think about the cost of being wrong before you act, switching thinking from automatic acceptance to deliberate judgment
The Judgment Framework is a set of three questions you ask before acting on any AI output
Severity — how bad the harm would be if the error actually occurs
Low detectability amplifies the cost even when severity and probability are individually moderate
Radiology AI with 87% sensitivity; severity high; detectability low (missed flag is absent signal); verdict: AI flag is not the final word
Legal contract AI; anchoring bias pulls attention toward AI-highlighted clauses
6% error at millions of posts = hundreds of thousands of wrong decisions daily
The habit of asking Q1 is the protection
Low-cost situations are real; recognising them lets you spend scrutiny where it matters

corpus_alignment.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus content. The reading faithfully condenses the corpus without introducing new claims.

soft_findings_summary.details: Term 'pharmacopeia' at line 75 of reading.md used without plain-language definition. Recommend: replace with 'the official drug reference guide (pharmacopeia)' or equivalent.

gold_example_check.notes: No gold example file found in reading gold-examples directory. Evaluation based on written standards in artifact-mix.json and SKILL.md.
